Sterilisation in Denmark 1929-67 analyze of Data tabels gathered by Lene Koch, and Rigsdagstidende Invalid counts from 1916, and population counts

Philip Brunsgaard

2026-05-09

#1. Invalids by group measured 1. feb 1916

#Reset so that the next chunk does mess up.
rm(list = ls())
gc()
##           used (Mb) gc trigger (Mb) max used (Mb)
## Ncells  619686 33.1    1419685 75.9   702074 37.5
## Vcells 1163247  8.9    8388608 64.0  1927849 14.8
data <- read.csv("C:/Users/phili/Documents/Invaliditetsgrupper1919 i forbindelse med invaliderente lov.csv",
                 stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# extract values as before
i <- which(data$invaliditetsgruppe_1919 == "men_women_total")
# remove excluded column and build df_plot
exclude <- "all_diseases"
cols <- setdiff(names(data), c("invaliditetsgruppe_1919", exclude))
vals <- as.numeric(unlist(data[i, cols]))
short_labels <- c("Mental or soul disability","Brain/Spine/Nerves","Neuroses","Eye","Ear","Respiratory","Heart/bloodvessels","Digestive","Urinary","Bones/Joints/Muscles","TB","Metabolic/Blood","Hernia","Tumors (fx Cancer)","Defects/deformities","Senility from age","Unspecified")
dataframe_plot <- data.frame(label = short_labels, value = vals, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

library(ggplot2)
## Warning: package 'ggplot2' was built under R version 4.5.3
Total_diseases_of_Men_Women_in_Danmark_from_1916_measures <- ggplot(dataframe_plot, aes(x = label, y = value)) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(x = "", y = "count of people", title = "Total diseases of Men & Women in Danmark from 1916 counts") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = -45, hjust = 0, vjust = 1, size = 9)
  )
Total_diseases_of_Men_Women_in_Danmark_from_1916_measures

#2. Age and Cause of Disability. Entire Country.
#These next two chunks are from Rigsdagstidende 1919-20 Tillæg A columns 3663-3670 which contains tabels of Age and Invalidity reasons for the whole country of Denmark. The count was made during the count of the people the 1. feb. 1916 due to "Invaliderente" law.

#Reset so that the next chunk does mess up.
rm(list = ls())
gc()
##           used (Mb) gc trigger  (Mb) max used  (Mb)
## Ncells 1305665 69.8    2619387 139.9  2436170 130.2
## Vcells 2304200 17.6    8388608  64.0  3568179  27.3
#Load data
library(tidyverse)
## Warning: package 'dplyr' was built under R version 4.5.3
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ dplyr     1.2.1     ✔ readr     2.2.0
## ✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.5     ✔ tibble    3.3.1
## ✔ purrr     1.2.1     ✔ tidyr     1.3.2
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
data <- read.csv("C:/Users/phili/Documents/Invaliditetsgrupper1919 i forbindelse med invaliderente lov.csv")

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
#Select a few disease columns to plot
library(tidyverse)

# keep only age groups (exclude totals for clearer comparison)
data_subset <- data %>%
  filter(invaliditetsgruppe_1919 %in% c(
    "men_15_til_19_year_olds",
    "men_20_til_24_year_olds",
    "men_25_til_29_year_olds",
    "men_30_til_34_year_olds",
    "men_35_til_39_year_olds",
    "men_40_til_44_year_olds",
    "men_45_til_49_year_olds",
    "men_50_til_54_year_olds",
    "men_55_til_59_year_olds",
    "men_60_til_64_year_olds",
    "women_15_til_19_year_olds",
    "women_20_til_24_year_olds",
    "women_25_til_29_year_olds",
    "women_30_til_34_year_olds",
    "women_35_til_39_year_olds",
    "women_40_til_44_year_olds",
    "women_45_til_49_year_olds",
    "women_50_til_54_year_olds",
    "women_55_til_59_year_olds",
    "women_60_til_64_year_olds"
  )) %>%
  select(
    invaliditetsgruppe_1919,
    sindssygdom_eller_aandssvaghed,
    sygdom_i_hjerne_rygmarv_og_periferiske_nerver,
    neuroser,
    øjensygdomme,
    øresygdomme,
    sygdom_i_aandedrætsorganerne_ikke_tuberkulose,
    sygdom_i_hjerte_og_blodkar,
    sygdom_i_fordøjelsesorganerne,
    sygdom_i_urinoganerne,
    sygdom_i_knogler_led_og_muskler,
    tuberkulose,
    stofskifte_og_blodsygdomme,
    brok,
    svulster_herunder_kræft,
    defekter_og_deformiteter,
    alderdomssvaghed,
    uangivet_og_daarlig_specificeret_aarsag,
    all_diseases
  )

data_subset <- data_subset %>%
  select(-all_diseases)

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)
# save original table order
group_order <- unique(data_subset$invaliditetsgruppe_1919)

data_long <- data_subset %>%
  mutate(
    sex = if_else(str_detect(invaliditetsgruppe_1919, "^men"), "Men", "Women"),
    age_group = str_remove(invaliditetsgruppe_1919, "^(men|women)_"),
    age_group = recode(age_group,
      "15_til_19_year_olds" = "15–19 years",
      "20_til_24_year_olds" = "20–24 years",
      "25_til_29_year_olds" = "25–29 years",
      "30_til_34_year_olds" = "30–34 years",
      "35_til_39_year_olds" = "35–39 years",
      "40_til_44_year_olds" = "40–44 years",
      "45_til_49_year_olds" = "45–49 years",
      "50_til_54_year_olds" = "50–54 years",
      "55_til_59_year_olds" = "55–59 years",
      "60_til_64_year_olds" = "60–64 years"
    ),
    age_group = factor(age_group, levels = c(
      "15–19 years", "20–24 years", "25–29 years", "30–34 years",
      "35–39 years", "40–44 years", "45–49 years", "50–54 years",
      "55–59 years", "60–64 years"
    ))
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -c(invaliditetsgruppe_1919, sex, age_group),
    names_to = "disease",
    values_to = "count"
  )

data_long <- data_long %>%
  mutate(
    disease = recode(disease,
      "sindssygdom_eller_aandssvaghed" = "Mental illness or intellectual disability",
      "sygdom_i_hjerne_rygmarv_og_periferiske_nerver" = "Brain, spinal cord and peripheral nerve diseases",
      "neuroser" = "Neuroses",
      "øjensygdomme" = "Eye diseases",
      "øresygdomme" = "Ear diseases",
      "sygdom_i_aandedrætsorganerne_ikke_tuberkulose" = "Respiratory diseases (excluding tuberculosis)",
      "sygdom_i_hjerte_og_blodkar" = "Heart and blood vessel diseases",
      "sygdom_i_fordøjelsesorganerne" = "Digestive diseases",
      "sygdom_i_urinoganerne" = "Urinary diseases",
      "sygdom_i_knogler_led_og_muskler" = "Bone, joint and muscle diseases",
      "tuberkulose" = "Tuberculosis",
      "stofskifte_og_blodsygdomme" = "Metabolic and blood diseases",
      "brok" = "Hernia",
      "svulster_herunder_kræft" = "Tumors including cancer",
      "defekter_og_deformiteter" = "Defects and deformities",
      "alderdomssvaghed" = "Senility",
      "uangivet_og_daarlig_specificeret_aarsag" = "Unspecified cause"
    )
  )

library(RColorBrewer)

graph_1916_diseases_men_women_denmark <- ggplot(data_long,
       aes(x = age_group,
           y = count,
           group = disease,
           color = disease)) +

  # regular diseases
  geom_line(
  data = filter(data_long, !disease %in% c(
    "Mental illness or intellectual disability",
    "Neuroses"
  )),
  alpha = 0.7,
  linewidth = 1
) +

geom_line(
  data = filter(data_long, disease == "Mental illness or intellectual disability"),
  linewidth = 1.8
) +
geom_line(
  data = filter(data_long, disease == "Neuroses"),
  linewidth = 1.8
) +

  facet_wrap(~ sex, ncol = 1) +

scale_color_manual(
  values = c(
    "Mental illness or intellectual disability" = "#E41A1C",
    "Neuroses" = "#377EB8",
    "Brain, spinal cord and peripheral nerve diseases" = "#4DAF4A",
    "Eye diseases" = "#984EA3",
    "Ear diseases" = "#FF7F00",
    "Respiratory diseases (excluding tuberculosis)" = "#FFFF33",
    "Heart and blood vessel diseases" = "#A65628",
    "Digestive diseases" = "#F781BF",
    "Urinary diseases" = "#999999",
    "Bone, joint and muscle diseases" = "#66C2A5",
    "Tuberculosis" = "#FC8D62",
    "Metabolic and blood diseases" = "#8DA0CB",
    "Hernia" = "#E78AC3",
    "Tumors including cancer" = "#A6D854",
    "Defects and deformities" = "#FFD92F",
    "Senility" = "#B3B3B3",
    "Unspecified cause" = "#1B9E77"
  )
) +

  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 8)
  ) +

  labs(
    title = "Disease patterns by age group (1916)",
    x = "Age group",
    y = "Count",
    color = "Disease"
  )
graph_1916_diseases_men_women_denmark
## Warning: Removed 4 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_line()`).

#3. Numbers of mentally ill by area and sex 1916

#Reset so that the next chunk does mess up.
rm(list = ls())
gc()
##           used (Mb) gc trigger  (Mb) max used  (Mb)
## Ncells 1566063 83.7    2619387 139.9  2619387 139.9
## Vcells 2736083 20.9    8388608  64.0  4409952  33.7
library(tidyverse)

df <- read_csv("C:/Users/phili/Desktop/Directory for Rstudio/Letsgo/data/Insane_and_feeble_sex.csv",
               col_types = cols())

plot_df <- df %>%
  rename(group = invaliditetsgruppe_1919,
         count = sindssygdom_eller_aandssvaghed) %>%
  mutate(is_total = str_detect(group, "_total$"),
         sex = case_when(
           str_starts(group, "men_") ~ "Men",
           str_starts(group, "women_") ~ "Women",
           TRUE ~ NA_character_),
         age = if_else(is_total, "Total",
                       str_remove(group, "^(men_|women_)") %>%
                         str_replace_all("_til_", "–") %>%
                         str_replace_all("_year_olds", " yrs"))) %>%
  filter(!is_total, !is.na(sex)) %>%
  mutate(count = as.numeric(str_replace_all(as.character(count), "[^0-9.-]", "")))


age_levels <- plot_df %>%
  distinct(age) %>%
  arrange(parse_number(age)) %>%
  pull(age)


ggplot(plot_df %>% mutate(age = factor(age, levels = age_levels)),
       aes(x = age, y = count, group = sex, color = sex)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) + geom_point(size = 2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Men" = "#1f78b4", "Women" = "#e31a1c")) +
  labs(title = "Counts by age (line)", x = "Age group", y = "Count", color = NULL) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
## Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0.
## ℹ Please use `linewidth` instead.
## This warning is displayed once per session.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.

#4. Numbers of Invalids by area and sex 1916
#These four chunks are also from the Rigsdagstidende 1919.

#Reset so that the next chunk does mess up.
rm(list = ls())
gc()
##           used (Mb) gc trigger  (Mb) max used  (Mb)
## Ncells 1631811 87.2    2619387 139.9  2619387 139.9
## Vcells 2864505 21.9    8388608  64.0  4409952  33.7
library(tidyverse)

df <- read_csv("C:/Users/phili/Documents/tabel_invalide_in_1916_in_relation_to_the_population_count_1916_csv.csv")
## New names:
## Rows: 18 Columns: 15
## ── Column specification
## ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Delimiter: "," chr
## (7): omraade1, ...10, ...11, ...12, ...13, ...14, ...15 dbl (8): maend,
## procentdel_maend, kvinder, procentdel_kvinder, i_alt, procen...
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data. ℹ
## Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
## • `` -> `...10`
## • `` -> `...11`
## • `` -> `...12`
## • `` -> `...13`
## • `` -> `...14`
## • `` -> `...15`
glimpse(df)
## Rows: 18
## Columns: 15
## $ omraade1                                                                       <chr> …
## $ maend                                                                          <dbl> …
## $ procentdel_maend                                                               <dbl> …
## $ kvinder                                                                        <dbl> …
## $ procentdel_kvinder                                                             <dbl> …
## $ i_alt                                                                          <dbl> …
## $ procentdel_af_totale_omraader                                                  <dbl> …
## $ folketaelinger_fra_danmarks_statistik_1916_s_54_til_55_Adolph_Jensen_har_brugt <dbl> …
## $ procent_invalide_i_alt_ud_af_befolkninger                                      <dbl> …
## $ ...10                                                                          <chr> …
## $ ...11                                                                          <chr> …
## $ ...12                                                                          <chr> …
## $ ...13                                                                          <chr> …
## $ ...14                                                                          <chr> …
## $ ...15                                                                          <chr> …
# rename long area names to shorter labels
df <- df %>%
  mutate(omraade1 = recode(omraade1,
    "koebenhavn_frederiksberg_og_koebstaeder_med_over_20000_indbyggere" = "Cities above 20k citizens",
    "andre_koebstaeder_og_landkommuner_med_bymaessige_bebyggelser_med_over_2000_indbyggere" = "Other cities above 2k",
    "andre_landkommuner_med_under_2000_indbyggere" = "Rural municipalities below 2k",
    "hele_landet" = "Entire country"
  ))


df_long <- df %>%
  select(omraade1, maend, kvinder, i_alt) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(maend, kvinder),
    names_to = "sex",
    values_to = "count"
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(count)) %>%
  mutate(
    sex = recode(sex,
      maend = "Men",
      kvinder = "Women"
    ),
    sex = factor(sex, levels = c("Men", "Women"))
  ) %>%
  mutate(omraade1 = factor(omraade1, levels = unique(df$omraade1)))

my_colours <- c("Men" = "#90EE90", "Women" = "#EE7600")

graph_number_of_invalids_by_area_and_sex_1916 <- ggplot(df_long, aes(x = omraade1, y = count, fill = sex)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = my_colours, labels = c("Men", "Women")) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Number of invalids by area and sex 1916", x = NULL, y = "Count", fill = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "top", axis.text.y = element_text(size = 6))
graph_number_of_invalids_by_area_and_sex_1916

#5. Invalid men and women by procent by area in DK 1916


#Reset so that the next chunk does mess up.
rm(list = ls())
gc()
##           used (Mb) gc trigger  (Mb) max used  (Mb)
## Ncells 1651680 88.3    2619387 139.9  2619387 139.9
## Vcells 2907520 22.2    8388608  64.0  4409952  33.7
df <- read_csv("C:/Users/phili/Documents/tabel_invalide_in_1916_in_relation_to_the_population_count_1916_csv.csv")
## New names:
## Rows: 18 Columns: 15
## ── Column specification
## ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Delimiter: "," chr
## (7): omraade1, ...10, ...11, ...12, ...13, ...14, ...15 dbl (8): maend,
## procentdel_maend, kvinder, procentdel_kvinder, i_alt, procen...
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data. ℹ
## Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
## • `` -> `...10`
## • `` -> `...11`
## • `` -> `...12`
## • `` -> `...13`
## • `` -> `...14`
## • `` -> `...15`
glimpse(df)
## Rows: 18
## Columns: 15
## $ omraade1                                                                       <chr> …
## $ maend                                                                          <dbl> …
## $ procentdel_maend                                                               <dbl> …
## $ kvinder                                                                        <dbl> …
## $ procentdel_kvinder                                                             <dbl> …
## $ i_alt                                                                          <dbl> …
## $ procentdel_af_totale_omraader                                                  <dbl> …
## $ folketaelinger_fra_danmarks_statistik_1916_s_54_til_55_Adolph_Jensen_har_brugt <dbl> …
## $ procent_invalide_i_alt_ud_af_befolkninger                                      <dbl> …
## $ ...10                                                                          <chr> …
## $ ...11                                                                          <chr> …
## $ ...12                                                                          <chr> …
## $ ...13                                                                          <chr> …
## $ ...14                                                                          <chr> …
## $ ...15                                                                          <chr> …
library(tidyverse)

# rename long area names to shorter labels
df <- df %>%
  mutate(omraade1 = recode(omraade1,
    "koebenhavn_frederiksberg_og_koebstaeder_med_over_20000_indbyggere" = "Cities above 20k citizens",
    "andre_koebstaeder_og_landkommuner_med_bymaessige_bebyggelser_med_over_2000_indbyggere" = "Other cities above 2k",
    "andre_landkommuner_med_under_2000_indbyggere" = "Rural municipalities below 2k",
    "hele_landet" = "Entire country"
  ))

# keep original order
df <- df %>% mutate(omraade1 = factor(omraade1, levels = unique(omraade1)))

library(RColorBrewer)

# select omraade1 and all procent* columns, pivot to long
procent_long <- df %>%
  select(omraade1, starts_with("procent")) %>%
  select(-procent_invalide_i_alt_ud_af_befolkninger) %>% 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -omraade1,
    names_to = "measure",
    values_to = "percent"
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(percent))   # drop NA rows

# nicer labels for measures
procent_long <- procent_long %>%
  mutate(measure = recode(measure,
    procentdel_maend = "% men",
    procentdel_kvinder = "% women",
    procentdel_af_totale_omraader = "% of total areas",
    procent_invalide_i_alt_ud_af_befolkninger = "% invalids of the pop."
  ))
# prepare: ensure measure factor order so sex measures plot together
procent_long <- procent_long %>%
  mutate(measure = factor(measure, levels = c("% men", "% women", "% of total areas", "% invalids of the pop.")))

# manual colours (one per measure)
cols <- RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(min(8, length(unique(procent_long$measure))), "Set2")
names(cols) <- unique(procent_long$measure)

# plot: grouped bars per area, faceted if too many measures
graph_invalid_men_and_women_by_procent_by_area_in_dk_1916 <- ggplot(
  procent_long %>% filter(measure != "% of total areas"),
  aes(x = omraade1, y = percent, fill = measure)
) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7, colour = "grey40") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cols) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Invalid men & women by % by area in DK 1916",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Percent",
    fill = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8), legend.position = "top")

graph_invalid_men_and_women_by_procent_by_area_in_dk_1916

#6. Distribution of invalids across area types in 1916 DK


#Reset so that the next chunk does mess up.
rm(list = ls())
gc()
##           used (Mb) gc trigger  (Mb) max used  (Mb)
## Ncells 1671317 89.3    2619387 139.9  2619387 139.9
## Vcells 2940531 22.5    8388608  64.0  4409952  33.7
df <- read_csv("C:/Users/phili/Documents/tabel_invalide_in_1916_in_relation_to_the_population_count_1916_csv.csv")
## New names:
## Rows: 18 Columns: 15
## ── Column specification
## ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Delimiter: "," chr
## (7): omraade1, ...10, ...11, ...12, ...13, ...14, ...15 dbl (8): maend,
## procentdel_maend, kvinder, procentdel_kvinder, i_alt, procen...
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data. ℹ
## Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
## • `` -> `...10`
## • `` -> `...11`
## • `` -> `...12`
## • `` -> `...13`
## • `` -> `...14`
## • `` -> `...15`
glimpse(df)
## Rows: 18
## Columns: 15
## $ omraade1                                                                       <chr> …
## $ maend                                                                          <dbl> …
## $ procentdel_maend                                                               <dbl> …
## $ kvinder                                                                        <dbl> …
## $ procentdel_kvinder                                                             <dbl> …
## $ i_alt                                                                          <dbl> …
## $ procentdel_af_totale_omraader                                                  <dbl> …
## $ folketaelinger_fra_danmarks_statistik_1916_s_54_til_55_Adolph_Jensen_har_brugt <dbl> …
## $ procent_invalide_i_alt_ud_af_befolkninger                                      <dbl> …
## $ ...10                                                                          <chr> …
## $ ...11                                                                          <chr> …
## $ ...12                                                                          <chr> …
## $ ...13                                                                          <chr> …
## $ ...14                                                                          <chr> …
## $ ...15                                                                          <chr> …
library(tidyverse)

# rename long area names to shorter labels
df <- df %>%
  mutate(omraade1 = recode(omraade1,
    "koebenhavn_frederiksberg_og_koebstaeder_med_over_20000_indbyggere" = "Cities above 20k citizens",
    "andre_koebstaeder_og_landkommuner_med_bymaessige_bebyggelser_med_over_2000_indbyggere" = "Other cities above 2k",
    "andre_landkommuner_med_under_2000_indbyggere" = "Rural municipalities below 2k",
    "hele_landet" = "Entire country"
  ))

# keep original order
df <- df %>% mutate(omraade1 = factor(omraade1, levels = unique(omraade1)))

library(RColorBrewer)

# select omraade1 and all procent* columns, pivot to long
procent_long <- df %>%
  select(omraade1, starts_with("procent")) %>%
  select(-procent_invalide_i_alt_ud_af_befolkninger) %>% 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -omraade1,
    names_to = "measure",
    values_to = "percent"
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(percent))   # drop NA rows

# nicer labels for measures
procent_long <- procent_long %>%
  mutate(measure = recode(measure,
    procentdel_maend = "% men",
    procentdel_kvinder = "% women",
    procentdel_af_totale_omraader = "% of total areas",
    procent_invalide_i_alt_ud_af_befolkninger = "% invalids of the pop."
  ))
# prepare: ensure measure factor order so sex measures plot together
procent_long <- procent_long %>%
  mutate(measure = factor(measure, levels = c("% men", "% women", "% of total areas", "% invalids of the pop.")))

# manual colours (one per measure)
cols <- RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(min(8, length(unique(procent_long$measure))), "Set2")
names(cols) <- unique(procent_long$measure)

# separate dataset for area share only (exclude total row)
area_share <- df %>%
  filter(omraade1 != "Entire country") %>%
  select(omraade1, procentdel_af_totale_omraader)

graph_distribution_of_invalids_across_area_types <- ggplot(area_share, aes(x = "All areas", y = procentdel_af_totale_omraader, fill = omraade1)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7, colour = "grey40") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of invalids across area types in 1916 DK",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Percent",
    fill = "Area"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11)

graph_distribution_of_invalids_across_area_types

#7. People counted and invalids by area (1916)


#Reset so that the next chunk does mess up.
rm(list = ls())
gc()
##           used (Mb) gc trigger  (Mb) max used  (Mb)
## Ncells 1671665 89.3    2619387 139.9  2619387 139.9
## Vcells 2943077 22.5    8388608  64.0  4409952  33.7
# Required packages
library(tidyverse)
library(scales)
## 
## Attaching package: 'scales'
## The following object is masked from 'package:purrr':
## 
##     discard
## The following object is masked from 'package:readr':
## 
##     col_factor
# Load your CSV (use forward slashes or escape backslashes)
df <- read_csv("C:/Users/phili/Desktop/Directory for Rstudio/Letsgo/data/compare_procent_of_invalids_men_and_women_with_total_population_per_area.csv")
## Rows: 4 Columns: 8
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ","
## chr (1): areas
## dbl (7): people_counted_from_danmarks_statistik_1916_page_54_to_55, invalid_...
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
# Ensure columns are named as expected; rename if necessary
df <- df %>%
  rename(
    area = areas,
    people = people_counted_from_danmarks_statistik_1916_page_54_to_55,
    invalid_men = invalid_men_in_1916,
    invalid_women = invalid_women_in_1916,
    total_invalids = total_invalids_men_women_1916
  )

# Add original order and grouping (adjust thresholds if needed)
df <- df %>%
  mutate(orig_order = row_number(),
         group = case_when(
           area == "All of Denmark" ~ "all_of_denmark",
           people > 20000 ~ "above_20k",
           people > 2000  ~ "above_2k",
           TRUE           ~ "below_2k"
         ))

# Compute the factor levels: group order as requested, keep original order within groups
area_levels <- df %>%
  arrange(factor(group, levels = c("above_20k", "above_2k", "below_2k", "all_of_denmark")),
          orig_order) %>%
  pull(area)

df <- df %>% mutate(area = factor(area, levels = unique(area_levels)))

# Prepare long format for invalids
invalid_long <- df %>%
  select(area, invalid_men, invalid_women) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("invalid"), names_to = "sex", values_to = "invalids") %>%
  mutate(sex = recode(sex, invalid_men = "Men", invalid_women = "Women"))

# Scale factor for secondary axis
mult <- max(df$people, na.rm = TRUE) / max((df$invalid_men + df$invalid_women), na.rm = TRUE)

# Plot with totals as outlined bars and numeric labels above totals
ggplot() +
  geom_bar(data = invalid_long, aes(x = area, y = invalids, fill = sex),
           stat = "identity", position = "stack", width = 0.6) +
  geom_bar(data = df, aes(x = area, y = people / mult),
           stat = "identity", fill = NA, colour = "black", size = 0.8, width = 0.9) +
  geom_text(data = df, aes(x = area, y = people / mult, label = comma(people)),
            vjust = -0.5, size = 3) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    name = "Number of invalids",
    labels = comma,
    sec.axis = sec_axis(~ . * mult, name = "Total people counted", labels = comma)
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Men" = "#1f77b4", "Women" = "#ff7f0e")) +
  labs(x = "Area", title = "People counted and invalids by area (1916)",
       caption = "Stacked invalids: men and women; outlined bars: total people") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.title.y.right = element_text(margin = margin(l = 10)),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off")  # allow labels outside plot area
## Warning in geom_bar(data = df, aes(x = area, y = people/mult), stat =
## "identity", : Ignoring unknown parameters: `size`

#8. People and invalids by area with percentage series 1916


#Reset so that the next chunk does mess up.
rm(list = ls())
gc()
##           used (Mb) gc trigger  (Mb) max used  (Mb)
## Ncells 1682043 89.9    2619387 139.9  2619387 139.9
## Vcells 2961816 22.6    8388608  64.0  4409952  33.7
# packages

library(tidyverse)
library(scales)

# load CSV (use your path)
df <- read_csv("C:/Users/phili/Desktop/Directory for Rstudio/Letsgo/data/compare_procent_of_invalids_men_and_women_with_total_population_per_area.csv")
## Rows: 4 Columns: 8
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ","
## chr (1): areas
## dbl (7): people_counted_from_danmarks_statistik_1916_page_54_to_55, invalid_...
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
# ensure areas is a factor in the desired order
df <- df %>% mutate(areas = factor(areas, levels = unique(areas)))

# long formats using original names
invalid_long <- df %>%
  select(areas, invalid_men_in_1916, invalid_women_in_1916) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(invalid_men_in_1916, invalid_women_in_1916),
               names_to = "sex", values_to = "invalids") %>%
  mutate(sex = recode(sex,
                      invalid_men_in_1916 = "Men",
                      invalid_women_in_1916 = "Women"))

pct_long <- df %>%
  select(areas, procent_of_total_population, procent_of_invalid_men_1916, procent_of_invalid_women_1916) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(procent_of_total_population, procent_of_invalid_men_1916, procent_of_invalid_women_1916),
               names_to = "series", values_to = "pct") %>%
  mutate(series = recode(series,
                         procent_of_total_population = "Pct of total population",
                         procent_of_invalid_men_1916 = "Pct invalid men",
                         procent_of_invalid_women_1916 = "Pct invalid women"))

# scale factors
mult_counts <- max(df$people_counted_from_danmarks_statistik_1916_page_54_to_55, na.rm = TRUE) /
               max((df$invalid_men_in_1916 + df$invalid_women_in_1916), na.rm = TRUE)
pct_to_count_mult <- (max(df$invalid_men_in_1916 + df$invalid_women_in_1916, na.rm = TRUE)) / 100

# plot
ggplot() +
  geom_col(data = invalid_long, aes(x = areas, y = invalids, fill = sex),
           position = "stack", width = 0.6) +
  geom_col(data = df, aes(x = areas, y = people_counted_from_danmarks_statistik_1916_page_54_to_55 / mult_counts),
           fill = NA, colour = "black", size = 0.8, width = 0.9) +
  geom_line(data = pct_long, aes(x = areas, y = pct * pct_to_count_mult, group = series, colour = series), size = 1) +
  geom_point(data = pct_long, aes(x = areas, y = pct * pct_to_count_mult, colour = series), size = 2) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    name = "Number of invalids",
    labels = comma,
    sec.axis = sec_axis(~ . / pct_to_count_mult, name = "Percent (%)", labels = function(x) paste0(round(x,1), "%"))
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Men" = "#1f77b4", "Women" = "#ff7f0e")) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("Pct of total population" = "#2ca02c",
                                 "Pct invalid men" = "#d62728",
                                 "Pct invalid women" = "#9467bd")) +
  labs(x = "Area", title = "People and invalids by area with percentage series",
       caption = "Stacked bars: invalid men/women; outlined bars: total people; lines: percentages") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
        axis.title.y.right = element_text(margin = margin(l = 10)),
        legend.title = element_blank())
## Warning in geom_col(data = df, aes(x = areas, y =
## people_counted_from_danmarks_statistik_1916_page_54_to_55/mult_counts), :
## Ignoring unknown parameters: `size`

#9. 1929 law
#This is tabel 1 in the back of Lene Koch's Tvangsterilisation i Danmark 1929-67, 2000. 

#Reset so that the next chunk does mess up.
rm(list = ls())
gc()
##           used (Mb) gc trigger  (Mb) max used  (Mb)
## Ncells 1682496 89.9    2619387 139.9  2619387 139.9
## Vcells 2962865 22.7    8388608  64.0  4409952  33.7
library(tidyverse)
data <- read.csv("C:/Users/phili/Desktop/Directory for Rstudio/Letsgo/data/Tabel_1_to_17_three_laws_involving_sterilisation.csv")
data <- read.csv("Tabel_1_to_17_three_laws_involving_sterilisation.csv", check.names = FALSE) #Making sure the names are not changed by R

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

data_subset <- data %>%
  filter(year >= 1930, year <= 1935) %>%
  select(year,  total_the_1929_law, women_the_1929_law, men_the_1929_law,   under_18_years_the_1929_law,    from_18_to_20_year_olds_the_1929_law,   from_21_to_30_year_olds_the_1929_law,   above_30_years_the_1929_law,    island_dioceses_the_1929_law,   jutland_institutions_the_1929_law,  institutional_residence_the_1929_law,   residence_under_other_care_the_1929_law,    residence_at_home_the_1929_law, eugenic_indication_the_1929_law,    non_eugenic_indication_the_1929_law)
#Total (1929) line
total <- data_subset %>%
  select(year, total_the_1929_law)

data_subset_no_total <- data_subset %>%
  select(-total_the_1929_law)


#convert to long format
#This is needed so that all selected columns become plottable lines.
library(tidyr)

data_long <- data_subset_no_total %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -year,
    names_to = "variable",
    values_to = "value"
  ) %>%
  mutate(variable = factor(variable, levels = names(data_subset_no_total)[-1])) #factor(...etc. locks ggplot ordering, and names(data_subset)[-1] preserves the original column order. 
data_long <- data_long %>%
  mutate(variable = factor(variable, levels = names(data_subset_no_total)[-1]))
#Split the plot into 4 groups
# Create grouping variable
data_long <- data_long %>%
  mutate(group = case_when(
    variable %in% c("women_the_1929_law", "men_the_1929_law") ~ "1: 3–4",
    
    variable %in% c("under_18_years_the_1929_law", "from_18_to_20_year_olds_the_1929_law", "from_21_to_30_year_olds_the_1929_law", "above_30_years_the_1929_law") ~ "2: 5–8",
    
    variable %in% c("island_dioceses_the_1929_law", "jutland_institutions_the_1929_law",
                    "institutional_residence_the_1929_law", "residence_under_other_care_the_1929_law",
                    "residence_at_home_the_1929_law") ~ "3: 9–13",
    
    variable %in% c("eugenic_indication_the_1929_law", "non_eugenic_indication_the_1929_law") ~ "4: 14–15"
  ))

# Ensure correct order of facets
data_long$group <- factor(data_long$group,
                          levels = c("1: 3–4",
                                     "2: 5–8",
                                     "3: 9–13",
                                     "4: 14–15"))
# Plot
library(ggplot2)

graph1929 <- ggplot(data_long, aes(x = year, y = value, color = variable)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
   geom_line(
  data = total,
  aes(x = year, y = total_the_1929_law, color = "Total (1929 law)"),
  inherit.aes = FALSE,
  linewidth = 1.3
) +
  facet_wrap(~ group, ncol = 2) +
    scale_color_manual(
  values = c(
    c(
  women_the_1929_law = "#1b9e77",
  men_the_1929_law = "#d95f02",
  under_18_years_the_1929_law = "#7570b3",
  from_18_to_20_year_olds_the_1929_law = "#e7298a",
  from_21_to_30_year_olds_the_1929_law = "#66a61e",
  above_30_years_the_1929_law = "#e6ab02",
  island_dioceses_the_1929_law = "#a6761d",
  jutland_institutions_the_1929_law = "#666666",
  institutional_residence_the_1929_law = "#1f78b4",
  residence_under_other_care_the_1929_law = "#b2df8a",
  residence_at_home_the_1929_law = "#fb9a99",
  eugenic_indication_the_1929_law = "#984ea3",
  non_eugenic_indication_the_1929_law = "#ff7f00",
  "Total (1929 law)" = "black"
),
    "Total (1929 law)" = "black"
  ),

  labels = c(
    women_the_1929_law = "Women",
    men_the_1929_law = "Men",
    under_18_years_the_1929_law = "Under 18 years",
    from_18_to_20_year_olds_the_1929_law = "18–20 years",
    from_21_to_30_year_olds_the_1929_law = "21–30 years",
    above_30_years_the_1929_law = "Above 30 years",
    island_dioceses_the_1929_law = "Island dioceses",
    jutland_institutions_the_1929_law = "Jutland institutions",
    institutional_residence_the_1929_law = "Stay at institution",
    residence_under_other_care_the_1929_law = "Stay under other care",
    residence_at_home_the_1929_law = "Stay at home",
    eugenic_indication_the_1929_law = "Eugenic indication",
    non_eugenic_indication_the_1929_law = "Non-eugenic indication",
    "Total (1929 law)" = "Total (1929 law)"
  )
) +
  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "No. of sterilisations",
    color = "Categories") +
  facet_wrap(~ group, ncol = 2, labeller = as_labeller(c(
  "1: 3–4" = "Sex",
  "2: 5–8" = "Age groups",
  "3: 9–13" = "Institutions & residency",
  "4: 14–15" = "Eugenic"
)))

graph1929

#10. 1934 law pt. 1
#The next three chunks with counts from the 1934 law are tabel 2-8 in the back of Lene Koch's Tvangsterilisation i Danmark 1929-67, 2000. 

#Reset so that the next chunk does mess up.
rm(list = ls())
gc()
##           used (Mb) gc trigger  (Mb) max used  (Mb)
## Ncells 1668340 89.1    2619387 139.9  2619387 139.9
## Vcells 2945133 22.5    8388608  64.0  4409952  33.7
library(tidyverse)
data <- read.csv("C:/Users/phili/Desktop/Directory for Rstudio/Letsgo/data/Tabel_1_to_17_three_laws_involving_sterilisation.csv")
data <- read.csv("Tabel_1_to_17_three_laws_involving_sterilisation.csv", check.names = FALSE) #Making sure the names are not changed by R

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

data_subset <- data %>%
  filter(year >= 1934, year <= 1968) %>%
  select(year, total_the_1934_law,  women_the_1934_law, men_the_1934_law,   under_18_years_old_the_1934_law,    from_18_to_20_years_old_the_1934_law,   from_21_to_30_years_old_the_1934_law,   above_30_years_the_1934_law,    island_dioceses_the_1934_law,   jutland_institutions_the_1934_law,  kellerske_jutland_institutions_the_1934_law,    ribe_jutland_institutions_the_1934_law, vodskov_jutland_institutions_the_1934_law,  institutional_residence_the_1934_law,   residence_under_other_care_the_1934_law,    residence_at_home_the_1934_law
)
#Total (1934) line
total <- data_subset %>%
  select(year, total_the_1934_law)

data_subset_no_total <- data_subset %>%
  select(-total_the_1934_law)


#convert to long format
#This is needed so that all selected columns become plottable lines.
library(tidyr)

data_long <- data_subset_no_total %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -year,
    names_to = "variable",
    values_to = "value"
  ) %>%
  mutate(variable = factor(variable, levels = names(data_subset_no_total)[-1])) #factor(...etc. locks ggplot ordering, and names(data_subset)[-1] preserves the original column order. 
data_long <- data_long %>%
  mutate(variable = factor(variable, levels = names(data_subset_no_total)[-1]))
#Split the plot into 4 groups
# Create grouping variable
data_long <- data_long %>%
  mutate(group = case_when(
    variable %in% c("women_the_1934_law", "men_the_1934_law") ~ "1: 17–18",
    
    variable %in% c("under_18_years_old_the_1934_law", "from_18_to_20_years_old_the_1934_law", "from_21_to_30_years_old_the_1934_law", "above_30_years_the_1934_law") ~ "2: 19–22",
    
    variable %in% c("island_dioceses_the_1934_law", "jutland_institutions_the_1934_law",
"kellerske_jutland_institutions_the_1934_law", "ribe_jutland_institutions_the_1934_law",
"vodskov_jutland_institutions_the_1934_law") ~ "3: 23–27",
    
    variable %in% c("institutional_residence_the_1934_law", "residence_under_other_care_the_1934_law", "residence_at_home_the_1934_law") ~ "4: 28–30"
  ))

# Ensure correct order of facets
data_long$group <- factor(data_long$group,
                          levels = c("1: 17–18",
                                     "2: 19–22",
                                     "3: 23–27",
                                     "4: 28–30"))
# Plot
library(ggplot2)

graph1934 <- ggplot(data_long, aes(x = year, y = value, color = variable)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
   geom_line(
  data = total,
  aes(x = year, y = total_the_1934_law, color = "Total (1934 law)"),
  inherit.aes = FALSE,
  linewidth = 1.3
) +
  facet_wrap(~ group, ncol = 2) +
    scale_color_manual(
  values = c(
    c(
  women_the_1934_law = "#1b9e77",
  men_the_1934_law = "#d95f02",
  under_18_years_old_the_1934_law = "#7570b3",
  from_18_to_20_years_old_the_1934_law = "#e7298a",
  from_21_to_30_years_old_the_1934_law = "#66a61e",
  above_30_years_the_1934_law = "#e6ab02",
  island_dioceses_the_1934_law = "#a6761d",
  jutland_institutions_the_1934_law = "#666666",
  kellerske_jutland_institutions_the_1934_law = "#1f78b4",
  ribe_jutland_institutions_the_1934_law = "#b2df8a",
  vodskov_jutland_institutions_the_1934_law = "#fb9a99",
  institutional_residence_the_1934_law = "#984ea3",
  residence_under_other_care_the_1934_law = "#ff7f00",
  residence_at_home_the_1934_law = "#e41a1c",
  "Total (1934 law)" = "black"
)),
  labels = c(
    women_the_1934_law = "Women",
    men_the_1934_law = "Men",
    under_18_years_old_the_1934_law = "Under 18 years",
    from_18_to_20_years_old_the_1934_law = "18–20 years",
    from_21_to_30_years_old_the_1934_law = "21–30 years",
    above_30_years_the_1934_law = "Above 30 years",
    island_dioceses_the_1934_law = "Island dioceses",
    jutland_institutions_the_1934_law = "Jutland institutions",
    kellerske_jutland_institutions_the_1934_law = "Kellerske, a J. Ins.",
    ribe_jutland_institutions_the_1934_law = "Ribe, a J. Ins.",
    vodskov_jutland_institutions_the_1934_law = "Vodskov, a J. Ins.",
    institutional_residence_the_1934_law = "Stay in institution",
    residence_under_other_care_the_1934_law = "Stay under other care",
    residence_at_home_the_1934_law = "Stay at home",
    "Total (1934 law)" = "Total (1934 law)"
  )
) +
  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "No. of sterilisations",
    color = "Categories") +
  facet_wrap(~ group, ncol = 2, labeller = as_labeller(c(
  "1: 17–18" = "Sex",
  "2: 19–22" = "Age groups",
  "3: 23–27" = "Institutions",
  "4: 28–30" = "Residence"
)))

graph1934

#11. 1934 pt. 2

#Reset so that the next chunk does mess up.
rm(list = ls())
gc()
##           used (Mb) gc trigger  (Mb) max used  (Mb)
## Ncells 1668442 89.2    2619387 139.9  2619387 139.9
## Vcells 2946949 22.5    8388608  64.0  4409952  33.7
library(tidyverse)
data <- read.csv("C:/Users/phili/Desktop/Directory for Rstudio/Letsgo/data/Tabel_1_to_17_three_laws_involving_sterilisation.csv")
data <- read.csv("Tabel_1_to_17_three_laws_involving_sterilisation.csv", check.names = FALSE) #Making sure the names are not changed by R

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

data_subset <- data %>%
  filter(year >= 1934, year <= 1968) %>%
  select(year, total_the_1934_law,  indication_eugenic_the_1934_law,    indication_not_eugenic_the_1934_law,    indication_eugenic_island_dioceses_the_1934_law,    indication_not_eugenic_island_dioceses_the_1934_law,    indication_eugenic_jutland_institutions_the_1934_law,   indication_not_eugenic_jutland_institutions_the_1934_law, weak_minded_IQ76_to_90_the_1934_law,  debile_IQ56_to_75_the_1934_law, imbeciles_IQ36_to_55_the_1934_law,  idiots_IQ0_to_35_the_1934_law,  weak_minded_island_dioceses_the_1934_law,   debile_island_dioceses_the_1934_law,    imbeciles_island_dioceses_the_1934_law, idiots_island_dioceses_the_1934_law,    weak_minded_jutland_institutions_the_1934_law,  debile_jutland_institutions_the_1934_law,   imbeciles_jutland_institutions_the_1934_law,    idiots_jutland_institutions_the_1934_law
)
#Total (1934) line
total <- data_subset %>%
  select(year, total_the_1934_law)

data_subset_no_total <- data_subset %>%
  select(-total_the_1934_law)


#convert to long format
#This is needed so that all selected columns become plottable lines.
library(tidyr)

data_long <- data_subset_no_total %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -year,
    names_to = "variable",
    values_to = "value"
  ) %>%
  mutate(variable = factor(variable, levels = names(data_subset_no_total)[-1])) #factor(...etc. locks ggplot ordering, and names(data_subset)[-1] preserves the original column order. 
data_long <- data_long %>%
  mutate(variable = factor(variable, levels = names(data_subset_no_total)[-1]))
#Split the plot into 4 groups
# Create grouping variable
data_long <- data_long %>%
  mutate(group = case_when(
    variable %in% c("indication_eugenic_the_1934_law",  "indication_not_eugenic_the_1934_law") ~ "1: 31–32",
    
    variable %in% c("indication_eugenic_island_dioceses_the_1934_law",  "indication_not_eugenic_island_dioceses_the_1934_law",  "indication_eugenic_jutland_institutions_the_1934_law", "indication_not_eugenic_jutland_institutions_the_1934_law") ~ "2: 33–36",
    
    variable %in% c("weak_minded_IQ76_to_90_the_1934_law",  "debile_IQ56_to_75_the_1934_law",   "imbeciles_IQ36_to_55_the_1934_law",    "idiots_IQ0_to_35_the_1934_law") ~ "3: 37–40",
    
    variable %in% c("weak_minded_island_dioceses_the_1934_law", "debile_island_dioceses_the_1934_law",  "imbeciles_island_dioceses_the_1934_law",   "idiots_island_dioceses_the_1934_law") ~ "4: 41–44",
    
    variable %in% c("weak_minded_jutland_institutions_the_1934_law",    "debile_jutland_institutions_the_1934_law", "imbeciles_jutland_institutions_the_1934_law",  "idiots_jutland_institutions_the_1934_law") ~ "5: 45–48"
  ))

# Ensure correct order of facets
data_long$group <- factor(data_long$group,
                          levels = c("1: 31–32",
                                     "2: 33–36",
                                     "3: 37–40",
                                     "4: 41–44",
                                     "5: 45–48"))
# Plot
library(ggplot2)

graph1934pt2 <- ggplot(data_long, aes(x = year, y = value, color = variable)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
   geom_line(
  data = total,
  aes(x = year, y = total_the_1934_law, color = "Total (1934 law)"),
  inherit.aes = FALSE,
  linewidth = 1.3
) +
  facet_wrap(~ group, ncol = 2) +
    scale_color_manual(
  values = c(
    c(
  indication_eugenic_the_1934_law = "#1b9e77",
  indication_not_eugenic_the_1934_law = "#d95f02",
  indication_eugenic_island_dioceses_the_1934_law = "#7570b3",
  indication_not_eugenic_island_dioceses_the_1934_law = "#e7298a",
  indication_eugenic_jutland_institutions_the_1934_law = "#66a61e",
  indication_not_eugenic_jutland_institutions_the_1934_law = "#e6ab02",
  weak_minded_IQ76_to_90_the_1934_law = "#a6761d",
  debile_IQ56_to_75_the_1934_law = "#666666",
  imbeciles_IQ36_to_55_the_1934_law = "#1f78b4",
  idiots_IQ0_to_35_the_1934_law = "#b2df8a",
  weak_minded_island_dioceses_the_1934_law = "#fb9a99",
  debile_island_dioceses_the_1934_law = "#984ea3",
  imbeciles_island_dioceses_the_1934_law = "#ff7f00",
  idiots_island_dioceses_the_1934_law = "#e41a1c",
  weak_minded_jutland_institutions_the_1934_law = "#CDB79E",
  debile_jutland_institutions_the_1934_law = "#66CDAA",
  imbeciles_jutland_institutions_the_1934_law = "#0000CD",
  idiots_jutland_institutions_the_1934_law = "#66CD00",
  "Total (1934 law)" = "black"
)),
  labels = c(
    indication_eugenic_the_1934_law = "Eugenic Ind.",
    indication_not_eugenic_the_1934_law = "Not eugenic Ind.",
    indication_eugenic_island_dioceses_the_1934_law = "Eugenic in island dioceses",
    indication_not_eugenic_island_dioceses_the_1934_law = "Not eugenic in island dioceses",
    indication_eugenic_jutland_institutions_the_1934_law = "Eugenic in Jutland inst.",
    indication_not_eugenic_jutland_institutions_the_1934_law = "Not eugenic in Jutland inst.",
    weak_minded_IQ76_to_90_the_1934_law = "IQ 76-90 weak minded",
    debile_IQ56_to_75_the_1934_law = "IQ 56-75 debile",
    imbeciles_IQ36_to_55_the_1934_law = "IQ 36-55 imbeciles",
    idiots_IQ0_to_35_the_1934_law = "IQ 0-35 idiots",
    weak_minded_island_dioceses_the_1934_law = "Weak minded in island dio.",
    debile_island_dioceses_the_1934_law = "Debile in island dio.",
    imbeciles_island_dioceses_the_1934_law = "Imbeciles in island dio.",
    idiots_island_dioceses_the_1934_law = "Idiots in island dio.",
    weak_minded_jutland_institutions_the_1934_law = "Weak minded in Jut. inst.",
    debile_jutland_institutions_the_1934_law = "Debile in Jut. Inst.",
    imbeciles_jutland_institutions_the_1934_law = "Imbeciles in Jut. Inst.",
    idiots_jutland_institutions_the_1934_law = "Idiots in Jut. inst.",
    "Total (1934 law)" = "Total (1934 law)"
  )
) +
  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "No. of sterilisations",
    color = "Categories") +
  facet_wrap(~ group, ncol = 2, labeller = as_labeller(c(
  "1: 31–32" = "Eugenic",
  "2: 33–36" = "Eugenics loc.",
  "3: 37–40" = "Danish IQ",
  "4: 41–44" = "IQ in Island dio.",
  "5: 45–48" = "IQ in Jut. inst."
)))

graph1934pt2

#12. 1934 pt. 3

#Reset so that the next chunk does mess up.
rm(list = ls())
gc()
##           used (Mb) gc trigger  (Mb) max used  (Mb)
## Ncells 1668641 89.2    2619387 139.9  2619387 139.9
## Vcells 2948349 22.5    8388608  64.0  4409952  33.7
library(tidyverse)
data <- read.csv("C:/Users/phili/Desktop/Directory for Rstudio/Letsgo/data/Tabel_1_to_17_three_laws_involving_sterilisation.csv")
data <- read.csv("Tabel_1_to_17_three_laws_involving_sterilisation.csv", check.names = FALSE) #Making sure the names are not changed by R

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

data_subset <- data %>%
  filter(year >= 1934, year <= 1968) %>%
  select(year, total_the_1934_law, weak_minded_IQ76_to_90_the_1934_law, yes_possibility_of_late_maturation_the_1934_law,    no_possibility_of_late_maturation_the_1934_law, undisclosed_possibility_of_late_maturation_the_1934_law,    reoperation_wanted_the_1934_law,    yes_discharge_planned_the_1934_law, undisclosed_discharge_planned_the_1934_law, island_dioceses_institution_IQ_above_75_at_institutions_the_1934_law,   the_three_jutland_institutions_IQ_above_75_at_institutions_the_1934_law,    kellerske_the_jutlandish_separately_IQ_above_75_at_institutions_the_1934_law,   ribe_the_jutlandish_separately_IQ_above_75_at_institutions_the_1934_law,    vodskov_the_jutlandish_separately_IQ_above_75_at_institutions_the_1934_law, satisfied_the_1934_law, dissatisfied_the_1934_law,  indifferent_the_1934_law,   undisclosed_concerning_satisified_or_not_the_1934_law)
#Total (1934) line
total <- data_subset %>%
  select(year, total_the_1934_law)

data_subset_no_total <- data_subset %>%
  select(-total_the_1934_law, -weak_minded_IQ76_to_90_the_1934_law)


#convert to long format
#This is needed so that all selected columns become plottable lines.
library(tidyr)

data_long <- data_subset_no_total %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -year,
    names_to = "variable",
    values_to = "value"
  ) %>%
  mutate(variable = factor(variable, levels = names(data_subset_no_total)[-1])) #factor(...etc. locks ggplot ordering, and names(data_subset)[-1] preserves the original column order. 
data_long <- data_long %>%
  mutate(variable = factor(variable, levels = names(data_subset_no_total)[-1]))
#Split the plot into 4 groups
# Create grouping variable
data_long <- data_long %>%
  mutate(group = case_when(
    variable %in% c("yes_possibility_of_late_maturation_the_1934_law",  "no_possibility_of_late_maturation_the_1934_law", "undisclosed_possibility_of_late_maturation_the_1934_law", "reoperation_wanted_the_1934_law") ~ "1: 49–52",
    
    variable %in% c("yes_discharge_planned_the_1934_law",   "undisclosed_discharge_planned_the_1934_law") ~ "2: 53–54",
    
    variable %in% c("island_dioceses_institution_IQ_above_75_at_institutions_the_1934_law", "the_three_jutland_institutions_IQ_above_75_at_institutions_the_1934_law",  "kellerske_the_jutlandish_separately_IQ_above_75_at_institutions_the_1934_law", "ribe_the_jutlandish_separately_IQ_above_75_at_institutions_the_1934_law", "vodskov_the_jutlandish_separately_IQ_above_75_at_institutions_the_1934_law") ~ "3: 55–59",
    
    variable %in% c("satisfied_the_1934_law",   "dissatisfied_the_1934_law",    "indifferent_the_1934_law", "undisclosed_concerning_satisified_or_not_the_1934_law") ~ "4: 60–63",
  ))

# Ensure correct order of facets
data_long$group <- factor(data_long$group,
                          levels = c("1: 49–52",
                                     "2: 53–54",
                                     "3: 55–59",
                                     "4: 60–63"))

total_group3 <- data_subset %>%
  select(year, weak_minded_IQ76_to_90_the_1934_law) %>%
  mutate(group = "3: 55–59")

# Plot
library(ggplot2)

graph1934pt3 <- ggplot(data_long, aes(x = year, y = value, color = variable)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
   geom_line(
  data = bind_rows(
    total %>% mutate(group = "1: 49–52"),
    total %>% mutate(group = "2: 53–54"),
    total %>% mutate(group = "4: 60–63")
  ),
  aes(x = year, y = total_the_1934_law, color = "Total (1934 law)"),
  inherit.aes = FALSE,
  linewidth = 1.3
) +
 geom_line(
  data = total_group3,
  aes(x = year, y = weak_minded_IQ76_to_90_the_1934_law, color = "weak_minded_IQ76_to_90_the_1934_law"),
  inherit.aes = FALSE,
  linewidth = 1.3
) +
  facet_wrap(~ group, ncol = 2, scales = "free_y") +
    scale_color_manual(
  values = c(
    c(
  yes_possibility_of_late_maturation_the_1934_law = "#1b9e77",
  no_possibility_of_late_maturation_the_1934_law = "#d95f02",
  undisclosed_possibility_of_late_maturation_the_1934_law = "#7570b3",
  reoperation_wanted_the_1934_law = "#e7298a",
  yes_discharge_planned_the_1934_law = "#66a61e",
  undisclosed_discharge_planned_the_1934_law = "#e6ab02",
  island_dioceses_institution_IQ_above_75_at_institutions_the_1934_law = "#a6761d",
  the_three_jutland_institutions_IQ_above_75_at_institutions_the_1934_law = "#FF1493",
  kellerske_the_jutlandish_separately_IQ_above_75_at_institutions_the_1934_law = "#1f78b4",
  ribe_the_jutlandish_separately_IQ_above_75_at_institutions_the_1934_law = "#b2df8a",
  vodskov_the_jutlandish_separately_IQ_above_75_at_institutions_the_1934_law = "#fb9a99",
  satisfied_the_1934_law = "#984ea3",
  dissatisfied_the_1934_law = "#ff7f00",
  indifferent_the_1934_law = "#e41a1c",
  undisclosed_concerning_satisified_or_not_the_1934_law = "#CDB79E",
  weak_minded_IQ76_to_90_the_1934_law = "darkslategrey",
  "Total (1934 law)" = "black"
)),
  labels = c(
    yes_possibility_of_late_maturation_the_1934_law = "Yes",
    no_possibility_of_late_maturation_the_1934_law = "No",
    undisclosed_possibility_of_late_maturation_the_1934_law = "Undisclosed",
    reoperation_wanted_the_1934_law = "Reoperation wanted",
    yes_discharge_planned_the_1934_law = "Yes",
    undisclosed_discharge_planned_the_1934_law = "Undisclosed",
    island_dioceses_institution_IQ_above_75_at_institutions_the_1934_law = "Island dioceses inst.",
    the_three_jutland_institutions_IQ_above_75_at_institutions_the_1934_law = "The three Jutland inst.",
    kellerske_the_jutlandish_separately_IQ_above_75_at_institutions_the_1934_law = "Kellerske Jut. inst.",
    ribe_the_jutlandish_separately_IQ_above_75_at_institutions_the_1934_law = "Ribe Jut. inst.",
    vodskov_the_jutlandish_separately_IQ_above_75_at_institutions_the_1934_law = "Vodskov Jut. inst.",
    satisfied_the_1934_law = "Satisfied",
    dissatisfied_the_1934_law = "Dissatisfied",
    indifferent_the_1934_law = "Indifferent",
    undisclosed_concerning_satisified_or_not_the_1934_law = "Undisclosed",
    weak_minded_IQ76_to_90_the_1934_law = "Total IQ above 75 (Weak minded)",
    
    "Total (1934 law)" = "Total (1934 law)"
  )
) +
  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "No. of sterilisations per year",
    color = "Categories") +
  facet_wrap(~ group, ncol = 2, scales = "free_y",
           labeller = as_labeller(c(
             "1: 49–52" = "Late-maturing, reoperation",
             "2: 53–54" = "Discharge planned from inst.",
             "3: 55–59" = "IQ above 75 per inst.",
             "4: 60–63" = "Satisfaction"
           )))

graph1934pt3

#13. 1935 law
#The next according to the 1935 law is tabel 10 in the back of Lene Koch's Tvangsterilisation i Danmark 1929-67, 2000. 

#Reset so that the next chunk does mess up.
rm(list = ls())
gc()
##           used (Mb) gc trigger  (Mb) max used  (Mb)
## Ncells 1669468 89.2    2619387 139.9  2619387 139.9
## Vcells 2950750 22.6    8388608  64.0  4409952  33.7
library(tidyverse)
data <- read.csv("C:/Users/phili/Desktop/Directory for Rstudio/Letsgo/data/Tabel_1_to_17_three_laws_involving_sterilisation.csv")
data <- read.csv("Tabel_1_to_17_three_laws_involving_sterilisation.csv", check.names = FALSE) #Making sure the names are not changed by R

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

data_long <- data %>%
  filter(year >= 1935, year <= 1968) %>%
  select(
    year,
    total_the_1935_law,
    women_the_1935_law,
    men_the_1935_law
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -year,
    names_to = "variable",
    values_to = "value"
  ) 
#Get the right order
data_long <- data_long %>%
  mutate(variable = factor(
    variable,
    levels = c(
      "total_the_1935_law",
      "women_the_1935_law",
      "men_the_1935_law"
    )
  ))

graph_gender_1935_law <- ggplot(data_long, aes(x = year, y = value, color = variable)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      total_the_1935_law = "black",
      women_the_1935_law = "#FF82AB",
      men_the_1935_law = "blue"
    ),
    labels = c(
      total_the_1935_law = "Total",
      women_the_1935_law = "Women",
      men_the_1935_law = "Men"
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Sterilisations acc. to gender under the 1935 law",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Number of people per year",
    color = "Category"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

graph_gender_1935_law

#14. Total every fifth year 1935 law
#The next according to the 1935 law with every fifth year counts are tabel 11-17 in the back of Lene Koch's Tvangsterilisation i Danmark 1929-67, 2000. 

#Reset so that the next chunk does mess up.
rm(list = ls())
gc()
##           used (Mb) gc trigger  (Mb) max used  (Mb)
## Ncells 1683944 90.0    2619387 139.9  2619387 139.9
## Vcells 2971875 22.7    8388608  64.0  4409952  33.7
library(tidyverse)
data <- read.csv("C:/Users/phili/Desktop/Directory for Rstudio/Letsgo/data/Tabel_1_to_17_three_laws_involving_sterilisation.csv")
data <- read.csv("Tabel_1_to_17_three_laws_involving_sterilisation.csv", check.names = FALSE) #Making sure the names are not changed by R

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

data_subset <- data %>%
  filter(year >= 1936, year <= 1966) %>%
  select(year, total_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law, epilepsy_diagnosis_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law,   mental_disorder_diagnosis_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law,    physical_illness_diagnosis_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law,   intellectual_disability_diagnosis_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law,    exhaustion_diagnosis_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law, other_diagnosis_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law, undisclosed_diagnosis_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law, amoral_social_and_moral_characteristic_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law,   asocial_or_criminal_social_and_moral_characteristic_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law,  poor_social_and_moral_characteristic_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law, other_social_and_moral_characteristic_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law,    undisclosed_social_and_moral_characteristic_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law,  doctor_initiative_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law,    institution_initiative_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law,   aid_of_mothers_initiative_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law,    social_committee_initiative_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law,  independent_initiative_initiative_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law,    other_authorities_initiative_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law, undisclosed_initiative_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law,   institution_support_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law,  self_support_support_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law, public_assistance_support_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law,    other_support_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law,    undisclosed_support_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law,  prison_place_of_residence_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law,    institution_place_of_residence_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law,   other_care_place_of_residence_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law,    home_place_of_residence_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law,  other_place_of_residence_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law, undisclosed_place_of_residence_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law,   eugenic_indication_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law,   partially_eugenic_indication_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law, non_eugenic_indication_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law,   undisclosed_indication_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law
)
#Total (1935) line
total <- data_subset %>%
  select(year, total_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law)

data_subset_no_total <- data_subset %>%
  select(-total_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law)



#convert to long format
#This is needed so that all selected columns become plottable lines.
library(tidyr)

data_long <- data_subset_no_total %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -year,
    names_to = "variable",
    values_to = "value"
  ) %>%
  mutate(variable = factor(variable, levels = names(data_subset_no_total)[-1])) #factor(...etc. locks ggplot ordering, and names(data_subset)[-1] preserves the original column order. 
data_long <- data_long %>%
  mutate(variable = factor(variable, levels = names(data_subset_no_total)[-1]))
#Split the plot into 6 groups
# Create grouping variable
data_long <- data_long %>%
  mutate(group = case_when(
    variable %in% c("epilepsy_diagnosis_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law", "mental_disorder_diagnosis_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law",  "physical_illness_diagnosis_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law", "intellectual_disability_diagnosis_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law",  "exhaustion_diagnosis_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law",   "other_diagnosis_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law", "undisclosed_diagnosis_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law") ~ "1: 73–79",
    
    variable %in% c("amoral_social_and_moral_characteristic_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law", "asocial_or_criminal_social_and_moral_characteristic_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law",    "poor_social_and_moral_characteristic_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law",   "other_social_and_moral_characteristic_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law",  "undisclosed_social_and_moral_characteristic_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law") ~ "2: 80–84",
    
    variable %in% c("doctor_initiative_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law",  "institution_initiative_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law", "aid_of_mothers_initiative_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law",  "social_committee_initiative_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law",    "independent_initiative_initiative_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law",  "other_authorities_initiative_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law",   "undisclosed_initiative_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law") ~ "3: 85–91",
    
    variable %in% c("institution_support_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law",    "self_support_support_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law",   "public_assistance_support_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law",  "other_support_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law",  "undisclosed_support_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law") ~ "4: 92–96",
    
    variable %in% c("prison_place_of_residence_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law",  "institution_place_of_residence_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law", "other_care_place_of_residence_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law",  "home_place_of_residence_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law",    "other_place_of_residence_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law",   "undisclosed_place_of_residence_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law") ~ "5: 97–101",
    
     variable %in% c("eugenic_indication_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law",    "partially_eugenic_indication_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law",   "non_eugenic_indication_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law", "undisclosed_indication_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law") ~ "6: 101–105"
  ))
# create total line and add it to all facet groups
total_long <- data_subset %>%
  select(year, total_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law) %>%
  rename(value = total_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law)

groups <- unique(data_long$group)

total_long <- expand.grid(
  year = unique(total_long$year),
  group = groups
) %>%
  left_join(total_long, by = "year") %>%
  mutate(variable = "Total (1935 law) every 5th year")

# Total line: exact same method as working 1934 graph
total_lines <- bind_rows(
  total %>% mutate(group = "1: 73–79"),
  total %>% mutate(group = "2: 80–84"),
  total %>% mutate(group = "3: 85–91"),
  total %>% mutate(group = "4: 92–96"),
  total %>% mutate(group = "5: 97–101"),
  total %>% mutate(group = "6: 101–105")
)

total_lines$group <- factor(total_lines$group,
                            levels = c("1: 73–79",
                                       "2: 80–84",
                                       "3: 85–91",
                                       "4: 92–96",
                                       "5: 97–101",
                                       "6: 101–105"))
#install.packages("zoo") 
library(zoo)
## Warning: package 'zoo' was built under R version 4.5.3
## 
## Attaching package: 'zoo'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     as.Date, as.Date.numeric
total_lines <- total_lines %>%
arrange(group, year) %>%
group_by(group) %>%
mutate(total_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = na.approx(total_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law, x = year, na.rm = FALSE)) %>%
fill(total_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law, .direction = "downup") %>%
ungroup()

#Clean data
data_long <- data_long %>%
  filter(!is.na(value)) %>%
  arrange(variable, year)

# Plot
library(ggplot2)

graph_1935_law_every_fifth_year <- ggplot(data_long, aes(x = year, y = value, color = variable)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2, na.rm = TRUE) +
  geom_line(
    data = total_lines,
    aes(x = year, y = total_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law, color = "Total (1935 law) every 5th year"),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    linewidth = 1.3,
    na.rm = TRUE
  ) +
    scale_color_manual(
  values = c(
  epilepsy_diagnosis_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "#E41A1C",
  mental_disorder_diagnosis_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "#377EB8",
  physical_illness_diagnosis_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "#4DAF4A",
  intellectual_disability_diagnosis_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "#984EA3",
  exhaustion_diagnosis_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "#FF7F00",
  other_diagnosis_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "#FFFF33",
  undisclosed_diagnosis_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "#A65628",

  amoral_social_and_moral_characteristic_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "#F781BF",
  asocial_or_criminal_social_and_moral_characteristic_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "#999999",
  poor_social_and_moral_characteristic_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "#66C2A5",
  other_social_and_moral_characteristic_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "#FC8D62",
  undisclosed_social_and_moral_characteristic_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "#8DA0CB",

  doctor_initiative_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "#E78AC3",
  institution_initiative_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "#A6D854",
  aid_of_mothers_initiative_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "#FFD92F",
  social_committee_initiative_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "#E5C494",
  independent_initiative_initiative_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "#B3B3B3",
  other_authorities_initiative_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "#1B9E77",
  undisclosed_initiative_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "#D95F02",

  institution_support_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "#7570B3",
  self_support_support_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "#E7298A",
  public_assistance_support_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "#66A61E",
  other_support_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "#E6AB02",
  undisclosed_support_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "#A6761D",

  prison_place_of_residence_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "#666666",
  institution_place_of_residence_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "#8DD3C7",
  other_care_place_of_residence_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "#FFFFB3",
  home_place_of_residence_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "#BEBADA",
  other_place_of_residence_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "#FB8072",
  undisclosed_place_of_residence_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "#80B1D3",

  eugenic_indication_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "#FDB462",
  partially_eugenic_indication_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "#B3DE69",
  non_eugenic_indication_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "#FCCDE5",
  undisclosed_indication_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "#BC80BD",
  "Total (1935 law) every 5th year" = "black"),
  
 labels = c(
  epilepsy_diagnosis_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "Epilepsy",
  mental_disorder_diagnosis_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "Mental disorder",
  physical_illness_diagnosis_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "Physical illness",
  intellectual_disability_diagnosis_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "Intellectual disability",
  exhaustion_diagnosis_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "Exhaustion",
  other_diagnosis_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "Other (diag.)",
  undisclosed_diagnosis_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "Undisclosed (diag.)",

  amoral_social_and_moral_characteristic_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "Amoral",
  asocial_or_criminal_social_and_moral_characteristic_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "Asocial or criminal",
  poor_social_and_moral_characteristic_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "Poor",
  other_social_and_moral_characteristic_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "Other (soc. & mor.)",
  undisclosed_social_and_moral_characteristic_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "Undisclosed (soc. & mor.)",

  doctor_initiative_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "Doctor",
  institution_initiative_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "Institution",
  aid_of_mothers_initiative_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "Aid of mothers",
  social_committee_initiative_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "Social committee",
  independent_initiative_initiative_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "Independent initiative",
  other_authorities_initiative_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "Other authorities",
  undisclosed_initiative_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "Undisclosed (init.)",

  institution_support_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "Institution",
  self_support_support_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "Self-support",
  public_assistance_support_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "Public assistance",
  other_support_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "Other (sup.)",
  undisclosed_support_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "Undisclosed (sup.)",

  prison_place_of_residence_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "Prison",
  institution_place_of_residence_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "Institution",
  other_care_place_of_residence_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "Other care",
  home_place_of_residence_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "Home",
  other_place_of_residence_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "Other (pla.)",
  undisclosed_place_of_residence_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "Undisclosed (pla.)",

  eugenic_indication_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "Eugenic",
  partially_eugenic_indication_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "Partially eugenic",
  non_eugenic_indication_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "Non-eugenic",
  undisclosed_indication_every_fifth_year_the_1935_law = "Undisclosed (ind.)",
    "Total (1935 law) every 5th year" = "Total (1935 law) every 5th year"
  )
) +
  labs(
    x = "Every fifth year counts",
    y = "No. of sterilisations",
    color = "Categories") +
  facet_wrap(~ group, ncol = 2, labeller = as_labeller(c(
  "1: 73–79" = "Diagnosis",
  "2: 80–84" = "Social & moral characteristic",
  "3: 85–91" = "Initiative",
  "4: 92–96" = "Support",
  "5: 97–101" = "Residence",
  "6: 101–105" = "Eugenic Indication"
)))

graph_1935_law_every_fifth_year
